chapter: Family as the First Power Structure
top_quotes: Because I'm not going to cut off the friendship just because she's on some bullshit.
I got to, like, test and confirm a lot of my theories.
Yeah, so I feel like for me, it was a learning opportunity.

I had someone else telling me that was toxic that I am too much in a rush to get everything done now and I want everything fast and in a minute I think they were just trying to slow me down so that I could do whatever they wanted me to do like it was something manipulative but I think they might be right because I feel like I have to have everything now if I can’t see it I can’t feel it if I’m not working like on level 10 trying to get to it then it doesn’t feel like it’s getting done.
I was like I will push through until I start results someone else toxic also told me that I don’t set boundaries and they were right.
I don’t sit boundaries in the traditional sense a lot of my boundaries I can be like indirect or I won’t announce them, but people will notice my presence missing or I might just mirror the person

what do you think that what do you think that okay so basically continuing the story so after all of that after the basically calling him out and like saying like do he even know who my family is and then calling him out basically he started getting this he started trying to get more disrespectful more aggressive more louder meanwhile i'm holding the same steady tone the whole time not letting up and and and because already previously prior to this earlier when we were early in the conversation i already got loud with him i already got aggressive with him i already got not really aggressive but i got loud and i got like you know in control like very strong way and strong armed my way into knowing that if i need to strong arm my strong arm my way into making him listen to me at first and like basically eating him up instructing him how a conversation works but me knowing that that didn't work the first time i'm not going to keep doing it again and again because i knew that was going to eat up at my energy so then i just kind of started flowing with it and so after i ate him up about the family comment he got disrespectful and he started trying to compare me to his mother or whatnot and i said
I get treated like an adult, and they look at me like I'm a troublesome one, but then they also respect me because I make shit happen.

And so, she's indoctrinated by them, and probably limited because she listens to them, and I'm not limited because I don't listen to them, and so even though I might be struggling, because I'm not within their little range of safety net, or control, or whatever.
However, I'm still making strides, and I feel like she's jealous, and mad, and I get it now, and now I'm not as angry anymore with her because I can see it from an even bigger picture than before.

passages.passage: Yeah, so I feel like for me, it was a learning opportunity. So, like, I got to see how she moves, how she operates, how people like her think, how people like her operate. I got to, like, test and confirm a lot of my theories. And, like, if she was ever confronted with a truth that she wouldn't be able to handle and she would die, there was accountability. And it's like, I just had months and months and months and months and months of research on how she operates so I can just know tip or tat. I feel like that's one of maybe the smartest things, even though it may have been, like, somewhat emotional draining. It's, like, one thing that I am able to do is, like, if you keep spinning the block on people, I feel like this is, like, the second person I spin the block on. It's, like, if you see a toxic person and you keep spinning the block on them, you start learning how to, like, work against that toxic shit. It's, like, you learn them from front to back, from back to front. So when you encounter other people like them, it's just automatic on how to respond to them, basically. And I feel like that's what I did for my ex and that's what I did for her, too. So if I talk to people sometimes, I keep them around just so I can know how to maneuver around them and eventually outmaneuver. And then eventually I gradually, after I've learned everything there is to learn, and I'm no longer benefiting from the situation anymore. Because I'm not going to cut off the friendship just because she's on some bullshit. I'm going to get whatever I can get out
best_sentences: Because I'm not going to cut off the friendship just because she's on some bullshit.
I got to, like, test and confirm a lot of my theories.
Yeah, so I feel like for me, it was a learning opportunity.

passages.passage: I think sometimes toxic people have a little bit of rightness that they be like my ex treat me like shit and I stay with her. It’s like why would anybody in the right man stay with someone or not show any anger or emotion or anything towards someone who’s blatantly doing everything to try to get a reaction or you know so I feel like she felt that emotional distance so that’s why she kept trying to call me gay cause like there has to be some sort of reason why I will stay with her despite her treating me so horribly And I don’t know at the time it’s because I was damaged. I had someone else telling me that was toxic that I am too much in a rush to get everything done now and I want everything fast and in a minute I think they were just trying to slow me down so that I could do whatever they wanted me to do like it was something manipulative but I think they might be right because I feel like I have to have everything now if I can’t see it I can’t feel it if I’m not working like on level 10 trying to get to it then it doesn’t feel like it’s getting done. It’s like I have no future vision. I was like I will push through until I start results someone else toxic also told me that I don’t set boundaries and they were right. I don’t directly set boundaries. I don’t sit boundaries in the traditional sense a lot of my boundaries I can be like indirect or I won’t announce them, but people will notice my presence missing or I might just mirror the person

passages.passage: And I don't see why my cousin is mad, because she's like, indoctrinated by them. She's like, the favorite, the goody one, the one that like, everyone loves, and it's like, the perfect one, the little angel that listens to everything they say, and they just give her whatever she wants. Like, here you go, baby. And then she gets treated like a baby, and I feel like, I don't get treated like a child. I get treated like an adult, and they look at me like I'm a troublesome one, but then they also respect me because I make shit happen. And they try to undermine me by talking shit about me, but then they end up getting proven wrong every time, so then it's like, they are just newly welcoming her into the group, so they think that, so she's some of this thing of talking shit about me, and then being proven wrong when I didn't do nothing to her. And so, she's indoctrinated by them, and probably limited because she listens to them, and I'm not limited because I don't listen to them, and so even though I might be struggling, because I'm not within their little range of safety net, or control, or whatever. However, I'm still making strides, and I feel like she's jealous, and mad, and I get it now, and now I'm not as angry anymore with her because I can see it from an even bigger picture than before. Her mom tells her to get what she can that. This is the amount of money that she has. It's in the budget and she'll try to do her best to like put each little tiny little bit of money and I'm different. If I see something I wanna, i'm gonna go

passages.passage: their own, you were welcomed and maintained a sense of personal superiority, knowing your value extended beyond their approval. --- Reflections Your journey reflects a balance of strength and adaptability. While you endured significant challenges, you found ways to assert your identity and remain resilient. The experiences also highlight how clout, loyalty, and perception play roles in shaping relationships and social dynamics. --- Transitioning to Section 4: Long-Term Impact and Lessons Learned In this final section, let’s reflect on how these experiences shaped you over time—how they influenced your identity, relationships, and outlook on life. --- Section 4: Long-Term Impact and Lessons Learned 1. Personal Growth: How did these experiences shape your sense of self? Did they change how you view resilience, conflict, or social dynamics? On one side I know that I'm strong on the other side I'm very much so frustrated that I had to go through these events in the first place and then to see how some solutions for one situation might not necessarily translate to success in another area. For example mastering these conflict in social hierarchy methods we're very effective in Middle School however in high school the environment was very much more volatile and the Dynamics very much more Dynamic and intricate. There were more expectations from people more things happening in the background and a lot more identity development collectively and individually which makes this experience very much more interesting. The same tools that helped me and middle school May have made things very much more harder for me in high school. Some people remember my reputation in middle school and try to help and hold me to that standard where I was at a lower Baseline so they can consistently position themselves above me. The roasting was quickly escalatory in inflammatory
best_sentences: On one side I know that I'm strong on the other side I'm very much so frustrated that I had to go through these events in the first place and then to see how some solutions for one situation might not necessarily translate to success in another area.
their own, you were welcomed and maintained a sense of personal superiority, knowing your value extended beyond their approval.
While you endured significant challenges, you found ways to assert your identity and remain resilient.

passages.passage: things very much more harder for me in high school. Some people remember my reputation in middle school and try to help and hold me to that standard where I was at a lower Baseline so they can consistently position themselves above me. The roasting was quickly escalatory in inflammatory as people's egos were much more sensitive. The fights carried much more weight. They're a lot more group dynamics. Nice time was over. Winning fights navigating social hierarchies and through group dynamics was not enough. Doing these things brought more attention good and bad which often lead to some more attention and more attention and more attention. It was a vicious cycle that could quickly become an engulfing fire that burns everything down Are there specific skills or traits you developed during this time that you still rely on today? I think I do rely a lot on maintaining the calmness and being able to sit in the fire a bit longer. The verbal sparring. The psychological games. The resorting to physical violence as the last resort however in a controlled aggression manner in order to maintain boundaries and control and command respect. 2. Relationships: How did these dynamics influence how you build and maintain relationships now? Do you approach trust or loyalty differently because of these experiences? I think that I'm very equipped with being able to build fast Rapport and relationships and identify people who I may have share commonalities with. However I also think that this has made me up keep more people at Arms distance and not being trusting of people. Have you kept any connections from that time, or did you leave most of those relationships behind? Everyone has been left. 3. Outlook on Life: Did these experiences change how you view the concept of
best_sentences: The resorting to physical violence as the last resort however in a controlled aggression manner in order to maintain boundaries and control and command respect.
things very much more harder for me in high school.
Some people remember my reputation in middle school and try to help and hold me to that standard where I was at a lower Baseline so they can consistently position themselves above me.

passages.passage: And I think that's why I'd be ready to be always winning right now. Think about it as a pattern, and if I did that as a child during sexual abuse, I'd probably flip that switch on everybody else. Like, I feel like that's why I've always been able to play the long game. Like, a lot of times, it would just be me holding out, holding out a reaction, holding out an emotional response, holding out even though I know that I'm mad, even though I know that I bother. And then, when I decide to let it out that I was mad the whole time or that I was upset the whole time, it's as if the other person's already weakened after trying me so long, so now I have the upper hand.
best_sentences: Like, I feel like that's why I've always been able to play the long game.

And then, when I decide to let it out that I was mad the whole time or that I was upset the whole time, it's as if the other person's already weakened after trying me so long, so now I have the upper hand.
And I think that's why I'd be ready to be always winning right now.

passages.passage: I have this cousin that has this overbearing mother that is highly critical and is probably the reason why my cousin, on top of pre-existing conditions, why she has impersonality disposition, why she is not assertive, doesn't stand up for herself, has a hard time with others, is a chronic people pleaser, and gets taken advantage of and does not handle conflict well is because of her mother. Me, on the other hand, I feel like we live in the same household. And I'm a fighter. I'm not going to lie. I'm just going to fight her. I just couldn't help it. I couldn't help it but to fight. But me getting older is learning how to fight less and learning how to strategically act more and how to fight in different ways while my cousin has been learning how to better managing people pleasing. And so I feel like there's some definitely some resentment there. I want you to simulate a roundtable of experts to talk about this situation. Who do you think that I should speak with?
best_sentences: I have this cousin that has this overbearing mother that is highly critical and is probably the reason why my cousin, on top of pre-existing conditions, why she has impersonality disposition, why she is not assertive, doesn't stand up for herself, has a hard time with others, is a chronic people pleaser, and gets taken advantage of and does not handle conflict well is because of her mother.
Me, on the other hand, I feel like we live in the same household.

But me getting older is learning how to fight less and learning how to strategically act more and how to fight in different ways while my cousin has been learning how to better managing people pleasing.

passages.passage: It's like he has this weird power dynamic that's, I feel like, borderline toxic because it's like he wants to keep being in control and I feel like there's a battle for dominance because he keeps talking to me crazy and I feel like he don't understand that if he keeps talking to me crazy he's not going to be able to talk to me at all.
best_sentences: It's like he has this weird power dynamic that's, I feel like, borderline toxic because it's like he wants to keep being in control and I feel like there's a battle for dominance because he keeps talking to me crazy and I feel like he don't understand that if he keeps talking to me crazy he's not going to be able to talk to me at all.

passages.passage: And I just think that it's hilarious, that it's like, I said what the fuck I said, like, when I confronted my family, like, I told them, like, I said what I said, y'all wanted to try to deflect, gaslight, and you're playing outdated games, baby, what you better realize is that I already see the board, so it's like, either you're gonna grow up and be a, be a adult about it, or you're gonna keep feeling small, and I feel like, they're gonna keep feeling small, because you have experience, something that you've done like that for years and years and years, you're gonna have to want to change it, you're gonna have to actually put in the work to change it, and in fact, that you're this old, always handing out advice and telling other people how to do things, but then you can't even be direct or handle confrontation, it's crazy.
best_sentences: And I just think that it's hilarious, that it's like, I said what the fuck I said, like, when I confronted my family, like, I told them, like, I said what I said, y'all wanted to try to deflect, gaslight, and you're playing outdated games, baby, what you better realize is that I already see the board, so it's like, either you're gonna grow up and be a, be a adult about it, or you're gonna keep feeling small, and I feel like, they're gonna keep feeling small, because you have experience, something that you've done like that for years and years and years, you're gonna have to want to change it, you're gonna have to actually put in the work to change it, and in fact, that you're this old, always handing out advice and telling other people how to do things, but then you can't even be direct or handle confrontation, it's crazy.

passages.passage: She gets met with, like, strong boundaries, like, then she realizes that I never have even mentioned any of this to anybody and nobody even confronts her about it, and there's just silence. It's just literally crickets for me. If we have any family in the family event, then I'll speak to everybody else and I just won't say anything to her. And then it's like, I would just completely act like whatever she said doesn't exist, like, so if we're all having a conversation and everybody's talking and having a conversation and she makes a statement, I just won't respond. And then I'll just talk about whatever I want to talk about without being rude, you know?
best_sentences: She gets met with, like, strong boundaries, like, then she realizes that I never have even mentioned any of this to anybody and nobody even confronts her about it, and there's just silence.

And then it's like, I would just completely act like whatever she said doesn't exist, like, so if we're all having a conversation and everybody's talking and having a conversation and she makes a statement, I just won't respond.

passages.passage: I feel like even in a really traumatic experience, like I had an event where I was being pressured into activities that were not very appropriate for someone at the age that I was actually engaging in, and it wasn't like I was being pressured with my will, I had a very strong will, so I was always like, no, no, no, no, no. And then eventually when I started seeing that the situation was escalating, I knew that if I kept saying no that they were going to keep pressuring me, so eventually I said yes. And then I would say no. Because I knew that saying yes, after saying no, was a lot more effective than just no outright because clearly my no's weren't working, but my yes after all those no's, it made them feel like they got what they wanted, but then after they felt like they got what they wanted, I controlled how much they got, and then after I controlled how much they got, I make them do all that motherfucking work for it again.
best_sentences: I feel like even in a really traumatic experience, like I had an event where I was being pressured into activities that were not very appropriate for someone at the age that I was actually engaging in, and it wasn't like I was being pressured with my will, I had a very strong will, so I was always like, no, no, no, no, no.

Because I knew that saying yes, after saying no, was a lot more effective than just no outright because clearly my no's weren't working, but my yes after all those no's, it made them feel like they got what they wanted, but then after they felt like they got what they wanted, I controlled how much they got, and then after I controlled how much they got, I make them do all that motherfucking work for it again.
And then eventually when I started seeing that the situation was escalating, I knew that if I kept saying no that they were going to keep pressuring me, so eventually I said yes.

passages.passage: Because I didn't get fired from all those jobs. Most of my jobs, I quit. Most of my relationships, I ended. Most of my friendships, I'm the one that left. I got tired of putting up with the bullshit. So I left. I said, I'm not putting up with this shit because I couldn't see myself dealing with it any longer. I could not see myself there. And I'll be seeing my coworkers and seeing people and they be exactly where they be at. And I just don't be seeing myself there. Like, I even had an ex. Like, I just wanted to run a test on myself to test, and I think that's how it's good, because I'd be testing myself in advance. And so even though I was testing myself to see where my limits were, if I can handle a relationship, if I can handle being cheated on, if I can handle bullshit, if I can handle a toxic person, I also was too running a background experiment, too, because I wanted to know that my hypothesis is that this nigga's not gonna change, that things are gonna get worse, that he's gonna take his brother back, that he keeps getting into these physical altercations that's affecting his criminal history, which is gonna affect his work, and he's already working the job that he doesn't like, so if he gets a criminal history, it's not gonna be good for his record. And everything that I predicted damn near came to pass, even worse than I could have imagined in some of the instances. But it's like, I was right. Shit didn't change. And then I realized a lot of people didn't. Now I'm starting to realize that a lot of people do not be changing. They be
best_sentences: And so even though I was testing myself to see where my limits were, if I can handle a relationship, if I can handle being cheated on, if I can handle bullshit, if I can handle a toxic person, I also was too running a background experiment, too, because I wanted to know that my hypothesis is that this nigga's not gonna change, that things are gonna get worse, that he's gonna take his brother back, that he keeps getting into these physical altercations that's affecting his criminal history, which is gonna affect his work, and he's already working the job that he doesn't like, so if he gets a criminal history, it's not gonna be good for his record.
Like, I just wanted to run a test on myself to test, and I think that's how it's good, because I'd be testing myself in advance.
Because I didn't get fired from all those jobs.

passages.passage: And so, I thought that my cousin was testing me this whole time because he kept fucking me, fucking me, fucking me, but really, I was just in control the whole time.
best_sentences: And so, I thought that my cousin was testing me this whole time because he kept fucking me, fucking me, fucking me, but really, I was just in control the whole time.

passages.passage: I feel like I’ve had a lot of weight on me ever since I was younger like I feel like from a very young age due to trauma and being in compromise predicament I had to make decisions for myself. I’ve even gotten kicked out of the first grade. This has been a very traumatic experience that I forgot about and ever since that point in time of my life, I’ve had to take extra control over myself and over my emotions and always stay in control. I will crash out and probably get in trouble and my life will get worse. I’m realizing now that this has been a tremendous pressure on me and now I am grown and I feel like I have issues because I have had to have so much self control and self discipline for so long. I don’t get to live a regular life. I don’t think I want a regular life. I always want more and better and something that will stimulate me more and better. I’ve tried to get off the bandwagon and I was miserable. What are your thoughts about the pressures that I experience? I’ve been bullied or at least people have tried. I have been neglected by the system, by my parents, my friends, etc. However I make the conscious decisions to not be a victim. I’ve always risen on top however, I’m tired now.
best_sentences: I’m realizing now that this has been a tremendous pressure on me and now I am grown and I feel like I have issues because I have had to have so much self control and self discipline for so long.
This has been a very traumatic experience that I forgot about and ever since that point in time of my life, I’ve had to take extra control over myself and over my emotions and always stay in control.
I always want more and better and something that will stimulate me more and better.

passages.passage: And maybe I shouldn't be mad at my aunt and at my cousin for being negative and being weak and just messy. It's like I just had to accept them for who they are and just know that's who they are and that's okay that they are that way. And I can't expect them to be better people. They are on their own journey. And I just have to protect my own energy and just know that I can't be around them if they're going to be negative.

passages.passage: I'll do my best to provide thoughtful responses based on general assumptions, but feel free to correct or add details where necessary. --- 1. Personal Challenges & Triggers Biggest emotional or mental barriers: Feeling overwhelmed by the amount of work required to clean and maintain organization, possibly leading to procrastination or avoidance. Struggles: Likely to struggle with motivation and focus more than the physical effort involved in cleaning. Potential conditions: May experience tendencies related to procrastination or executive dysfunction, which could stem from stress, anxiety, or difficulty maintaining routines. --- 2. Current Habits & Patterns Past cleaning methods: Likely have tried sporadic deep-cleaning sessions that felt effective but weren’t sustainable long-term. Checklists or task batching may have worked briefly. Existing routines: May have some informal routines, such as tidying up when things become visibly cluttered, but might lack a consistent system. Task prioritization: Cleaning may often take a backseat to other responsibilities like work, personal interests, or relaxation time. --- 3. Living Environment Size and layout: Likely living in a moderate-sized space such as an apartment or shared home, where space efficiency might be an issue. Living situation: Might live alone or with a few others, which could mean personal accountability is key, but external factors (roommates, family habits) may also play a role. Environmental factors: Possible clutter from daily activities, digital devices, or limited storage solutions. --- 4. Energy & Time Management Available time: Likely to have limited time daily due to other commitments, making short, focused cleaning sessions more practical. Peak energy times: Mornings or evenings might work better for cleaning depending on your personal productivity rhythm. Competing priorities: Work, hobbies, or social activities might make it challenging to consistently dedicate time to cleaning. --- 5. Motivation & Accountability Motivation to clean: Aesthetic appeal and mental clarity could

passages.passage: And then... What happened? Then what happened? So... What happened? I'm trying to think. Fuck. Oh yeah. So one day, Jalen... I think he's just jealous. Because one day, he just found a reason to start an argument. He got water on me, but didn't want me to put water back on him. It wasn't even water. It was like a couple of drops. Bitch, don't put a couple of drops on me if you don't put a couple of drops on you. And so, he did it to me, and he thought I was gonna be scared. And so he said he was gonna beat my ass if I do it. So I did it anyways. He hit me in my face. And I just walked away. And then I went to the store, because I was hungry. I went to the store, and Jalen... It was Jalen... Jalen's older brother and a friend. Jalen's older brother's friend. They were at the store. And at first, they saw me, and... They saw like one drop of blood on my shirt. And they were like, oh, he really did it. He really did it. And they were just laughing. Like, trying to clown me about it. Like I was weak. And I just looked at them. And I just stared at them. No facial expressions, no emotions, nothing like that. I just stared at them. And then I paid for my stuff. And then we all walked back to Jalen's house and played the game.
best_sentences: And then I went to the store, because I was hungry.
Because one day, he just found a reason to start an argument.
He got water on me, but didn't want me to put water back on him.
